meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve Stuck with Basic Apps
content.seo_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop charging hourly for commodity work. Transition to value-based SaaS pricing by packaging your AI-driven features into tiered subscription models. Use Antigravity to scaffold robust billing logic and Claude Code to iterate on deployment, transforming your basic app portfolio into high-margin, recurring revenue engines that clients actually want to buy.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: staring at a list of CRUD apps that look great on a portfolio but fail to move the needle on your bank account. You are stuck in the 'basic app' trap, trading hours for dollars on projects that don't scale. The marketplace is flooded with developers, but it is starving for architects who understand the intersection of AI utility and business profitability.

The shift isn't about writing more code; it's about engineering more value. By implementing strategic SaaS pricing models, you transform from a freelancer into a partner. We will use Antigravity to architect your billing infrastructure and Claude Code to accelerate your development lifecycle, ensuring you ship premium, revenue-ready applications in record time.

steps.title: Define Your Value Tiers
steps.description: Stop guessing your prices. Define three distinct tiers: Starter (MVP utility), Professional (Workflow automation), and Enterprise (Custom AI integration). This anchors client expectations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a JSON schema for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers: Starter, Professional, and Enterprise. Include fields for monthly_price, feature_list (as an array), and rate_limit_per_month.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a pricing_tiers.json file based on the schema provided by Antigravity'

steps.title: Architect the Billing Logic
steps.description: Use Antigravity to map out the billing flow. We need to ensure the logic handles subscription state, upgrades, and grace periods without bloating your codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript interface for a BillingManager class that handles Stripe checkout session creation and subscription status updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement the BillingManager class in src/services/billing.ts using the generated interface'

steps.title: Integrate AI Usage Tracking
steps.description: SaaS pricing for AI apps must be usage-based. Integrate a token-counting utility that triggers a webhook when a user hits their tier limit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function for Express that calculates OpenAI token usage per request and checks it against the user's current subscription tier limit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create middleware/usageTracker.ts and apply it to the main API router'

steps.title: Automate Deployment Pipelines
steps.description: Efficiency is the freelancer's best friend. Set up a CI/CD flow that ensures your pricing tiers are validated before every production push.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow YAML file that runs unit tests on the pricing logic before deploying to Vercel.

steps.title: Finalize Client Dashboard
steps.description: Build a clean UI that displays the pricing tiers. Transparency increases conversion rates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React pricing table component that fetches the pricing tiers from the backend and highlights the 'Professional' tier as the recommended option.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create src/components/PricingTable.tsx and integrate it into the dashboard'

pro_tips: Always offer a 'Founding Member' lifetime deal for the first 10 users to validate your pricing model quickly.
Never hardcode prices; use a remote config or a database table so you can update pricing without redeploying code.
Use Stripe's 'Customer Portal' instead of building your own subscription management UI to save 20+ hours of development.
Implement 'soft limits' in your AI app that allow users to exceed their tier limit for a small overage fee rather than breaking their workflow.
Track 'time-to-first-value' for your users; if it's over 60 seconds, your onboarding is too complex for a paid tier.
content.next_steps: Deploy your pricing-enabled application to a staging environment, run a load test using your Claude Code scripts, and invite your first three beta testers to validate your new subscription tiers.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop building basic apps and start building profitable SaaS businesses. Apply these pricing strategies today to reclaim your time and scale your freelance income. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
